channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump names Andrew Ferguson as head of Federal Trade Commission to replace Lina Khan
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has named Andrew Ferguson to be the next chair of the Federal Trade Commission.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Report on attempts to kill Trump urges Secret Service to limit protection of foreign leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A congressional task force investigating the attempts to kill Donald Trump during his presidential campaign is recommending changes to the Secret Service, including protecting fewer foreign leaders during the height of election season and considering moving the agency out of the Ho
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he was 'stupid' not to put his name on pandemic relief checks like Trump did
item.description: President Joe Biden says he was “stupid” not to put his own name on pandemic relief checks in 2021, like Donald Trump had done in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump is returning to the world stage. So is his trolling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s recent dinner with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and his visit to Paris for the reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral were not just exercises in policy and diplomacy. They were also prime trolling opportunities for Trump.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI leader says it's 'concerning' how little his agency knows about mysterious drones seen over New Jersey
item.description: A top FBI leader revealed the agency knows concerningly little about the mysterious, SUV-sized drones that have been seen hovering over New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House task force cites 'various failures' ahead of 'preventable' Trump shooting
item.description: The House select committee that investigated the two assassination attempts against President-elect Trump released a report on Tuesday that found the July 13 attack at Trump’s rally in Butler, Pa., was “preventable and should not have happened.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump shooting task force says DHS, Secret Service haven't produced docs on golf course incident
item.description: The task force investigating the assassination attempts of President-elect Trump released its final report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House advances annual defense policy bill, teeing up final vote
item.description: The House advanced the annual defense policy bill on Tuesday, teeing up the mammoth measure for a final vote later this week. The chamber voted 211-207 along party lines to adopt the rule — which governs debate for legislation — for the sprawling bill, formally opening up debate in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. McConnell reportedly fine after falling during GOP lunch
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell fell during a GOP lunch on Tuesday and was met with a wheelchair and emergency crews in his office on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Yorkers could receive 'inflation refund' check from state: Here's how much
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of New Yorkers would receive hundreds of dollars in an “inflation refund” payment from the state next year under a new proposal from Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: House panel holds drone hearing
item.description: Capitol Hill has taken notice of recent, mysterious drone sightings across the Northeast and is hoping to get to the bottom of the mystery.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Biden stakes out economic legacy at Brookings as Trump readies takeover
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday made the case for the strength of his economic legacy as he prepared to leave office next month, arguing President-elect Trump would be inheriting a desirable situation and warning of the risks of the incoming administration’s proposals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi says she backs generational change on committees, but there are exceptions
item.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) said this week that she supports the push for younger Democrats to replace veteran lawmakers at the top of committees — but there is at least one glaring exception to the rule.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will support new Syrian leaders who protect women and renounce terrorism
item.description: The Biden administration says the United States will recognize and support a new Syrian government that renounces terrorism, destroys chemical weapons stocks and protects the rights of minorities and women.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin, Welch unveil bill placing term limits on Supreme Court
item.description: West Virginia Sen. Joe Manchin (I) and Vermont Sen. Peter Welch (D) have introduced a resolution to impose 18-year term limits on Supreme Court justices, which would require some turnover on the high court every two years.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: House Republican eyes Medicare, Social Security for DOGE cuts
item.description: Rep. Mark Alford (R-Mo.) is floating Medicare and Social Security as potential areas of focus for President-elect Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names several picks for State Department roles
item.description: In several posts on Truth Social, Trump shared the new positions to work under his nominated Secretary of State Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to president
item.description: “Alina has been a tireless advocate for Justice, a fierce Defender of the Rule of Law, and an invaluable Advisor to my Campaign and Transition Team,” Trump said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Trump criticizes Chronister after sheriff's withdrawal, days after praising him
item.description: President Donald Trump challenged reports of Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister's DEA withdrawal, saying he didn't want the sheriff days his cabinet days after nominating him.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Montana lawmakers cross the aisle to block a trans bathroom ban in the state capitol
item.description: Some Republicans joined Democrats in rejecting the measure, which would have restricted bathroom access. Rep. Zooey Zephyr, Montana's first openly transgender legislator, won a second term last month.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota budget update shows $1.1B less than prior forecast
item.description: The economic report released Wednesday morning by state budget officials projects a downturn, with only a $616 million cushion expected by the end of the next budget period. The new data will guide lawmakers when budget deliberations begin in January.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Brian Bakst, MPR News
